perturbation.original_text: (11) The provisions of this agreement relating to confidentiality shall terminate two years from the date hereof.
perturbation.changed_text: (11) The provisions of this agreement relating to confidentiality shall terminate five years from the date hereof.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction. It changes the termination date of the confidentiality agreement from two years to five years. This will contradict the reference to the termination date in Section (6).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (6) At any time upon the Company’s request, all Confidential Evaluation Material (and all copies, summaries, and notes of the contents or parts thereof) shall be returned or, if you so choose, destroyed and not retained by you or your representatives in any form or for any reason; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall be obligated to retain a copy of the Confidential Evaluation Material for a period of one year after the termination of this agreement for auditing purposes. written certification to that effect will be sent by you to the Company within 30 days of such request; provided, however, that you and your representatives shall not be obligated to return or destroy the Confidential Evaluation Material if, and to the extent, otherwise required by any applicable law, regulation, policy or procedures relating to the retention, back-up storage or automatic archiving of files and data. Any Confidential Evaluation Material that is retained by you or your representatives for the sole purpose of compliance with such law, regulation, policy or procedures shall not be used for any purpose other than to evidence compliance therewith and such retained material and information shall continue to be subject to the confidentiality obligations set forth in this letter agreement, which obligations shall survive any termination hereof. All the Confidential Evaluation Material will be and remains the property of the Company. 2
perturbation.justification: The original text specified a two-year termination for confidentiality, while the changed text extends it to five years. The contradicted text in section (6) states that the Confidential Evaluation Material must be retained for one year after the termination of the agreement for auditing purposes. By changing the termination date from two years to five years, the time for which the material must be retained after the original termination date is now different, creating a direct inconsistency and in-text contradiction.

perturbation.original_text: (13) “You” used alone shall mean Silver Lake Management Company, L.L.C. and its officers, employees and Affiliates.
perturbation.changed_text: (13) “You” used alone shall mean Silver Lake Management Company, L.L.C. only.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates an in-text contradiction by narrowing the definition of “You.” Originally, it included Silver Lake, its officers, employees, and affiliates. Now, it refers to only Silver Lake Management Company, L.L.C., creating a conflict with sections that grant rights and impose obligations on officers, employees, and Affiliates as representatives of 'You'. The contradiction arises in paragraphs (2) and (10) where the responsibilities and limitations extend to 'your representatives' implicitly referencing officers, employees, and Affiliates.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text narrows the definition of 'You' from including Silver Lake, its officers, employees, and affiliates to only Silver Lake Management Company, L.L.C. This creates a direct contradiction with paragraph (2) in the contradicted text, which states that 'You and your representatives agree...' and later defines 'your representatives' to include officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors. Since the changed definition of 'You' no longer includes these representatives, it contradicts the clause in paragraph (2) that imposes obligations and grants rights to these representatives under the umbrella of 'You', causing an in-text contradiction. This aligns with the perturbation explanation and definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction'.

perturbation.original_text: (3) In the event you or your representatives receive a request to disclose Confidential Evaluation Material pursuant to any court order or legal process, you or your representatives will give the Company prompt written notice thereof so that the Company may seek an appropriate protective order. You and your representatives agree to cooperate (at the Company’s expense) as reasonably requested by the Company in its efforts to maintain the confidentiality of such Confidential Evaluation Material. If you or your representatives are ultimately required to disclose such Confidential Evaluation Material, you or your representatives shall disclose only so much thereof as necessary to comply with such court order or legal process.
perturbation.changed_text: (3) In the event you receive a request to disclose Confidential Evaluation Material pursuant to any court order or legal process, you will give the Company prompt written notice thereof so that the Company may seek an appropriate protective order. You agree to cooperate (at the Company’s expense) as reasonably requested by the Company in its efforts to maintain the confidentiality of such Confidential Evaluation Material. If you are ultimately required to disclose such Confidential Evaluation Material, you shall disclose only so much thereof as necessary to comply with such court order or legal process. Your representatives are not bound by this term.
perturbation.explanation: This modification creates a contradiction. It removes the obligation for 'your representatives' to notify the Company in case of a disclosure request and explicitly states they are not bound by this term. This contradicts the initial sections of the contract that bind 'your representatives' to the same confidentiality obligations as Silver Lake, creating uncertainty about the enforcement of confidentiality. This also directly contradicts the term in Section (2).
perturbation.contradicted_text: (2) You and your representatives agree that the Confidential Evaluation Material will be used solely for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction. You also agree that you and your officers, employees, financing sources, consultants, Affiliates, agents and advisors, herein collectively referred to as “ your representatives ,” will not disclose or permit the disclosure of any of the Confidential Evaluation Material now or hereafter received or obtained from the Company or its representatives to any third party or otherwise use or permit the use of the Confidential Evaluation Material in any way detrimental to the Company, except as required by court order or legal process, without the prior written consent of the Company, provided, however, that any such information may be disclosed to such of your representatives who need to know such information for the purpose of evaluating the proposed transaction and who are advised of this agreement and agree to keep such information confidential and to be bound by this agreement to the same extent as if they were parties hereto, it being understood that you shall be responsible for any breach of this agreement by your representatives; provided, however, that you shall not be responsible for any failure to comply with the terms of this agreement by any of your representatives that (i) is not one of your officers, employees or Affiliates and (ii) has signed an undertaking in favor of the Company and you to the effect of Annex A hereto.
perturbation.justification: The changed text explicitly states that 'your representatives are not bound by this term,' which directly contradicts the contradicted text, where 'your representatives' are bound by the agreement to the same extent as the contracting party and the contracting party is responsible for their breach. This creates a clear in-text contradiction as per the provided definition of 'Inconsistencies - In Text Contradiction' since the changed section provides conflicting obligations.

perturbation.original_text: (7) The following information provided by the Company shall not be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material: (A) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of an improper disclosure by you or your representatives; (B) any information which becomes legally available to you from a source other than the Company that is not, to your knowledge after reasonable inquiry, bound by a confidentiality obligation to the Company; (C) any information that is already in your possession as of the date hereof or (D) any information that is independently developed by you without use of or reference to the Confidential Evaluation Material.
perturbation.changed_text: (7) The following information provided by the Company shall not be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material: (A) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of an improper disclosure by you; (B) any information which becomes legally available to you from a source other than the Company that is not, to your knowledge after reasonable inquiry, bound by a confidentiality obligation to the Company; (C) any information that is already in your possession as of the date hereof or (D) any information that is independently developed by you without use of or reference to the Confidential Evaluation Material. Your representatives are fully responsible for keeping the shared information from the company secret.
perturbation.explanation: By removing "your representatives" from (A), and adding "Your representatives are fully responsible for keeping the shared information from the company secret" it is creating an in-text contradiction because, if the representatives improperly share it, it will still be considered confidential evaluation material, even though a general statement of responsibility is issued. This directly contradicts clause (A), thus a contradiction is created.
perturbation.contradicted_text: (7) The following information provided by the Company shall not be deemed Confidential Evaluation Material: (A) any information that is or becomes generally available to the public other than as a result of an improper disclosure by you; (B) any information which becomes legally available to you from a source other than the Company that is not, to your knowledge after reasonable inquiry, bound by a confidentiality obligation to the Company; (C) any information that is already in your possession as of the date hereof or (D) any information that is independently developed by you without use of or reference to the Confidential Evaluation Material. Your representatives are fully responsible for keeping the shared information from the company secret. (8) Neither this letter agreement nor any action taken in connection with this letter agreement will give rise to any obligation on the part of either you or the Company (a) to engage in any discussions or negotiations with the other party or with any of the other party’s representatives, or (b) to pursue or enter into any transaction of any nature with the other party. The parties acknowledge and agree that neither shall have any legally binding commitment for a transaction unless set forth in a separate written agreement that is executed and delivered by both of them.
perturbation.justification: The original text stated that information would not be deemed confidential if it became public through an improper disclosure by 'you or your representatives.' The changed text removes 'your representatives' from this exception and adds a sentence stating that 'Your representatives are fully responsible for keeping the shared information from the company secret.' This creates an in-text contradiction because if the representatives improperly share the information (making it publicly available), the original clause (A) in the contradicted text states that such information is NOT confidential, while the added sentence implies that representatives sharing information improperly *should* keep it confidential, thereby still classifying it as confidential. This is a direct conflict regarding whether information disclosed by representatives is considered confidential or not under clause (7)(A) within the contradicted text.
